analysis:
1. Fed Rate Path: April and June Hold Expectations Surge

The dominant story is markets pricing out any near-term Fed cuts. "No change after April 2026 meeting" jumped +13.5pp this week to 91%, while the mirror market — "25 bps cut after April" — fell -12pp to just 5%. The June hold market moved in lockstep, up +12pp to 62%. The full-year cut distribution confirms the hawkish shift: "1 cut in 2026" sits at 30%, "0 cuts" at 23.3%, and "2 cuts" at 23% — with the modal outcome now either zero or one cut. The March meeting is already a foregone conclusion at 99.6% for no change.

2. NVIDIA Consolidates Market Cap Dominance

NVIDIA's probability of being the largest company by June 30 rose +6pp this week to 79%, on top of a +7.5pp monthly gain. It already sits at 98.8% to hold the top spot through March 31. Alphabet is the clearest loser in this framing: its June 30 odds fell -4.8pp this week to just 7.8%, and its March 31 odds are at 0.6%. Apple ticked up slightly (+1.3pp weekly) to 0.4% for March 31 — irrelevant in magnitude but directionally consistent with a broader non-NVIDIA rotation story. No other company comes close to challenging NVIDIA's near-term position.

3. US Recession and Macro Tail Risk

The US recession by end-2026 market sits at 34% with a -3pp monthly drift and flat week — notable as a stable but elevated baseline. "No Fed rate cuts in 2026" at 23.3% (down -2pp this week) suggests markets haven't fully abandoned easing but are pricing meaningful risk of prolonged hold. The "12 or more cuts" market at 1.8% and the "3 cuts" market falling -2pp to 11% this week reinforce that aggressive easing scenarios are being systematically repriced away. Recession risk and rate stasis are now co-existing in the market's central scenario.

4. MicroStrategy Bitcoin Sale Risk Remains Negligible

All MicroStrategy Bitcoin sale markets remain deeply skeptical of any forced selling. The "sells by March 31" market is at 0.8%, "by June 30" at 3.4%, and "by December 31, 2026" at 12% — the latter falling -2.5pp this week and -10pp this month. The already-resolved "sells any Bitcoin in 2025" market sits at 100%, confirming the question resolved as "no sale." The Bank of Japan holds at 97.7% for no change at its March meeting, adding to a global central bank pause narrative that indirectly supports crypto collateral stability.

5. Key Shifts This Week

The single largest mover is "No change after April 2026 meeting" at +13.5pp to 91%, directly mirrored by "25 bps cut after April" at -12pp to 5% — a decisive repricing of the April meeting in a single week. "No change after June 2026 meeting" added +12pp to 62%, extending the hold narrative two meetings out. NVIDIA's June 30 top market cap position gained +6pp to 79%, while Alphabet shed -4.8pp to 7.8% for the same date. The "Fed Cut–Pause–Pause" sequence market confirmed at +4.3pp to 99.5%, essentially closing out the March meeting narrative and shifting full attention to whether the pause extends through mid-year.
